memoryArtifacts.headline: First view of a tomb of a wife of a Percy believed to be Idonea Clifford in Beverly Minster (Parish Church of St. John and St. Martin)
memoryArtifacts.description:
First view of a tomb of a wife of a Percy believed to be Idonea Clifford in Beverly Minster (Parish Church of St. John and St. Martin) 

Currently it is believed to be the location of the remains of Lady Idonea Clifford Percy (died 1365), wife of the second Lord Percy; although earlier it was attributed to Lady Eleanor Percy, wife of the First Lord Percy.

memoryArtifacts.headline: Beverly Minster (Parish Church of St. John and St. Martin)
memoryArtifacts.description:
Beverly Minster (Parish Church of St. John and St. Martin) 

Remains of many prominent families buried here, including the Percys.

Image adapted from Bond, "Gothic Architecture," p. 177.

Legendary founder was  the bishop  St. John of Beverley (d. 721, canonized 1037) whose relics are in this minster. Church on the site damaged by fire in  20 October, 1188 (MacMahon, K.A., The pictorial history of Beverley Minster. A Brief History. London: Pitkin Pictorials, 1967, p. 8).

The large tower fell in 1210's.

Present choir rebuilt ca 1220-60. Nave rebuilt in early 14th century.

http://www.medart.pitt.edu/image/England/Beverley/Minster/mainbev-minster.html

popularStory.description:

zh.lifeSummary: 當Idonea de Clifford於1300年出生在英國賀福爾時，她的父親Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford26歲，母親Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles24歲。她於大約1314年與Henry Percy在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1365年8月24日在英國賀福爾克利福德享年65歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁貝弗利 聖 約翰 煙雨 聖 馬丁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Idonea
meanings: An old baptismal name derived from Latin idoneus, fit, meet, proper.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Idonea de Clifford was born in 1300, in Herefordshire, England, her father, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, was 26 and her mother, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, was 24. She married Henry Percy about 1314, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 24 August 1365, in Clifford, Herefordshire, England, at the age of 65, and was buried in Beverley St John with St Martin, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Henry Percy environ 1314, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 août 1365, à Clifford, Herefordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterrée à Beverley St John with St Martin, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Idonea de Cliffordが1300年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Cliffordは26歳，母Maud de Clare Baroness de Wellesは24歳であった。約1314年にイギリスヨークシュアイアでHenry Percyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1365年8月24日にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアクリフォードで65歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングベバーリー スト ジョン ウィス スト マルティンにて埋葬された。

ko.Under the Percy Shrine, Beverley Minster: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 베벌리 스트 존 위스 스트 마틴
ko.lifeSummary: Idonea de Clifford(이)가 1300년 영국 허퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1314년 영국 요르크실르에서 Henry Percy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1365년 8월 24일 영국 허퍼드실르 클리퍼드에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 베벌리 스트 존 위스 스트 마틴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Idonea de Clifford nasceu em 1300, em Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry Percy aproximadamente 1314, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de agosto de 1365, em Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, com 65 anos, e foi sepultada em Beverley St John with St Martin, Yorkshire East Riding, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Under the Percy Shrine, Beverley Minster: бивирли ст джон вит ст мартинь, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Idonea de Clifford родилась в 1300 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry Percy около 1314 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 24 августа 1365 г. в местности клиффорд, хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 65 лет и была похоронена в местности бивирли ст джон вит ст мартинь, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Idonea de Clifford nació en 1300, en Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Clifford 1st Baron de Clifford, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Maud de Clare Baroness de Welles, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Henry Percy aproximadamente 1314, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 24 de agosto de 1365, en Clifford, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultada en Beverley St John with St Martin, Yorkshire East Riding, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
